title: Samsung Makes Big Move Into Humanoid Robots— Hires Former Hyundai Robotics Leader to Lead the Charge
excerpt: Samsung Electronics has tapped former Hyundai Motor Co. Ltd. robotics executive Lee Dongkun to help turn humanoid robots and physical AI into a major growth...
content_html:
Shomik Sen Bhattacharjee

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 7:31 AM CDT 5 min read

Benzinga and Yahoo Finance LLC may earn commission or revenue on some items through the links below.

Samsung Electronics has tapped former Hyundai Motor Co. Ltd. robotics executive Lee Dongkun to help turn humanoid robots and physical AI into a major growth business, placing its newly created robotics division directly under the company's chief executive.

Former Hyundai Executive Leads Robotics Strategy

According to a Reuters report, Samsung said on Tuesday that its RX, or "Robotics eXperience," division will oversee long-term strategy, core technology development and commercialization. Lee, an executive vice president, will lead its Robotics Strategy Team after previously directing Hyundai's robotics plans, including work involving Boston Dynamics.

Don't Miss:

  • A single bad hire can set a startup back years. Here are the 5 hires founders most often misjudge — and why

  • Still Learning the Market? These 50 Must-Know Terms Can Help You Catch Up Fast

The appointment gives Samsung an executive familiar with one of the humanoid industry's most advanced players. Hyundai recently moved to take full control of Boston Dynamics, whose electric Atlas robot is expected to enter Hyundai's Georgia manufacturing operations by 2028.

Benzinga reached out to Samsung Electronics for comment, but did not receive an immediate response.

Samsung Expands Global Physical AI Research

Samsung will also establish robotics research hubs in the U.S., China and Japan, using local engineering talent and technology ecosystems to strengthen its global capabilities. The company said advances in "physical AI," which allows machines to perceive and act in real-world environments, are making commercial robotics increasingly viable.

Trending: Avoid the #1 Investing Mistake: How Your 'Safe' Holdings Could Be Costing You Big Time

The expansion follows Samsung's growing investments in the sector. The company became Rainbow Robotics' largest shareholder in December after investing 267 billion won, then worth about $181 million. Samsung has also backed AI robotics developer Skild AI.

Humanoid Plans Target Factories Before Consumers

Samsung said in January that it wanted "tangible" results from humanoid robotics this year. It plans to deploy humanoids first at manufacturing sites to improve productivity before expanding into homes and retail businesses. The company has separately outlined plans to convert its manufacturing network into "AI-driven factories" by 2030.

The wider market is accelerating. The International Federation of Robotics recorded 542,000 new industrial robot installations in 2024, more than double the level a decade earlier, with Asia accounting for 74% of deployments.

Benzinga 和 Yahoo Finance LLC 可能通过以下链接赚取某些项目的佣金或收入。

三星电子聘请了前现代汽车公司。 Ltd. 机器人高管 Lee Dongkun 帮助将人形机器人和物理人工智能转变为主要的增长业务，并将其新创建的机器人部门直接置于该公司首席执行官的领导之下。

现代汽车前高管领导机器人战略

据路透社报道，三星周二表示，其 RX（即“机器人体验”）部门将负责监督长期战略、核心技术开发和商业化。 执行副总裁李将领导其机器人战略团队，此前他曾指导现代汽车的机器人计划，包括涉及波士顿动力公司的工作。

不要错过：

  • 一次糟糕的招聘可能会让一家初创企业倒退数年。 以下是创始人最常误判的 5 名员工——以及原因
  • 还在学习市场吗？ 这 50 个必须知道的术语可以帮助您快速跟上

此次任命为三星带来了一位熟悉人形机器人行业最先进参与者之一的高管。 现代汽车最近完全控制了波士顿动力公司，该公司的电动 Atlas 机器人预计将于 2028 年进入现代汽车在佐治亚州的制造业务。

本辛加联系了三星电子请其置评，但没有立即得到回复。

三星扩大全球物理人工智能研究

三星还将在美国、中国和日本建立机器人研究中心，利用当地的工程人才和技术生态系统来增强其全球能力。 该公司表示，“物理人工智能”的进步使机器能够在现实世界环境中感知和行动，这使得商业机器人变得越来越可行。

趋势：避免第一大投资错误：你的“安全”持仓可能会让你付出巨大的代价

此次扩张是继三星在该领域不断增加投资之后进行的。 该公司于去年 12 月投资 2670 亿韩元（当时价值约 1.81 亿美元），成为 Rainbow Robotics 的最大股东。 三星还支持人工智能机器人开发商 Skild AI。

人形机器人计划先瞄准工厂，再瞄准消费者

三星一月份表示，希望今年人形机器人技术取得“有形”成果。 该公司计划首先在制造工厂部署类人机器人，以提高生产力，然后再扩展到家庭和零售企业。 该公司还单独制定了到 2030 年将其制造网络转变为“人工智能驱动的工厂”的计划。

更广阔的市场正在加速发展。 国际机器人联合会记录，2024 年新增工业机器人安装量为 542,000 台，是十年前水平的两倍多，其中亚洲占部署量的 74%。
